# Kalico — TMC Stepper Driver Support

**Type:** knowledge
**Summary:** Kalico firmware TMC stepper driver support — six driver ICs (TMC2130, TMC2208, TMC2209, TMC2240, TMC2660, TMC5160/2160), per-driver capabilities, configuration parameters, Kalico-specific additions vs. stock Klipper, and sensorless homing infrastructure.
**Tags:** #software #drivers #reference #kalico #klipper
**Status:** draft
**Updated:** 2026-04-08
**Source:** auto-generated by opencode/claude-opus-4.6 from Kalico GitHub (KalicoCrew/kalico, commit 3b7b793) and docs.kalico.gg
**Related:** [[TMC2209]], [[TMC2240]], [[TMC5160A]], [[TMC4671-LA]], [[TMC-Driver-Comparison]], [[AN-001-SpreadCycle-Parameterization]], [[AN-002-StallGuard2-CoolStep]], [[AN-015-StealthChop-Performance]]

---

## Overview

Kalico is a Klipper fork providing stepper motor driver support for six Trinamic TMC ICs. Each driver is implemented as a `klippy/extras/tmc*.py` module exposing chip-specific configuration through Klipper's `[tmc*]` config sections. Kalico extends stock Klipper with additional short-circuit protection tuning (TMC5160/2160), current range control (TMC2240), and native homing-current management (`home_current` / `current_change_dwell_time`) that eliminates the need for sensorless-homing macros.

This page summarizes which TMC chips Kalico drives, their capabilities and config parameters, and what Kalico adds beyond upstream Klipper.

---

## Supported Drivers

| Driver | Interface | Current Control | StealthChop | CoolStep | Vhigh | WaveTable | Sensorless Homing | StallGuard |
|--------|-----------|----------------|-------------|----------|-------|-----------|-------------------|------------|
| TMC2130 | SPI | sense_resistor | ✓ | ✓ (Vcoolthrs) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (diag0/diag1) | SG2 |
| TMC2208 | UART | sense_resistor | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |
| TMC2209 | UART | sense_resistor | ✓ | ✓ (Vcoolthrs) | ✗ | ✗ | ✓ (diag_pin) | SG4 |
| TMC2240 | SPI or UART | Rref-based | ✓ | ✓ (Vcoolthrs) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (diag0/diag1) | SG4 |
| TMC2660 | SPI | sense_resistor + vsense | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ (physical pin only) | SG (RDSEL) |
| TMC5160 | SPI | sense_resistor + GLOBALSCALER | ✓ | ✓ (Vcoolthrs) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ (diag0/diag1) | SG2 |

> **TMC2160** is a pin-compatible alias for TMC5160 in Kalico — uses the same driver module (`tmc5160.py`), same config section `[tmc5160]`.

---

## Common Infrastructure

All TMC drivers share a common infrastructure layer in `tmc.py` providing:

| Helper Class | Function |
|---|---|
| MCU_TMC_SPI / MCU_TMC_SPI_chain | SPI transport (single and daisy-chain) |
| MCU_TMC_uart | UART transport |
| TMCCommandHelper | G-code commands: DUMP_TMC, SET_TMC_FIELD, SET_TMC_CURRENT, INIT_TMC |
| BaseTMCCurrentHelper | Current calculation from run_current / hold_current / sense_resistor |
| TMCStealthchopHelper | StealthChop enable/disable, threshold management |
| TMCVcoolthrsHelper | CoolStep velocity threshold |
| TMCVhighHelper | High-velocity fullstep / CoolStep disable thresholds |
| TMCWaveTableHelper | Custom sine wave table loading |
| TMCVirtualPinHelper | Virtual endstop pins for sensorless homing |

---

## Per-Driver Configuration

### TMC2130

**Interface:** SPI
**Config section:** `[tmc2130 stepper_x]`
**Source:** `klippy/extras/tmc2130.py`

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| spi_speed | int | 4000000 | SPI clock speed (Hz) |
| run_current | float | required | Motor run current (A RMS) |
| hold_current | float | run_current | Standstill current (A RMS) |
| sense_resistor | float | 0.110 | External sense resistor (Ω) |
| stealthchop_threshold | int | 0 | Velocity threshold for StealthChop→SpreadCycle transition (0 = disabled) |
| home_current | float | — | Reduced current for homing (Kalico-specific) |
| current_change_dwell_time | float | 0.5 | Delay (s) after current change before homing move |
| driver_MSLUT* | int | — | Microstep wave table entries (MSLUT0–MSLUT7) |
| driver_W* | int | — | Wave table segment widths |
| driver_X* | int | — | Wave table segment transitions |
| driver_START_SIN* | int | — | Wave table start sine values |
| driver_IHOLDDELAY | int | 8 | Run-to-hold current transition delay |
| driver_TPOWERDOWN | int | 0 | Standstill power-down delay |
| driver_TBL | int | 1 | Blanking time |
| driver_TOFF | int | 4 | Chopper off-time |
| driver_HEND | int | 7 | Hysteresis end value |
| driver_HSTRT | int | 0 | Hysteresis start value |
| driver_TPFD | int | 4 | Passive fast decay time |
| driver_CHM | int | 0 | Chopper mode (0=SpreadCycle, 1=constant TOFF) |
| driver_VHIGHFS | int | 0 | High-velocity fullstep mode |
| driver_VHIGHCHM | int | 0 | High-velocity chopper mode |
| driver_DISS2G | int | 0 | Disable short-to-GND protection |
| driver_DISS2VS | int | 0 | Disable short-to-VS protection |
| driver_PWM_AUTOSCALE | int | 1 | StealthChop auto amplitude scaling |
| driver_PWM_AUTOGRAD | int | 1 | StealthChop auto gradient adaptation |
| driver_PWM_FREQ | int | 1 | PWM frequency selection |
| driver_PWM_GRAD | int | 0 | PWM gradient |
| driver_PWM_AMPL | int | 128 | PWM amplitude |
| driver_SGT | int | 0 | StallGuard2 threshold (−64 to +63) |
| driver_SEMIN | int | 0 | CoolStep minimum SG reading (0 = CoolStep off) |
| driver_SEUP | int | 0 | CoolStep current increment |
| driver_SEMAX | int | 0 | CoolStep upper threshold |
| driver_SEDN | int | 0 | CoolStep current decrement |
| driver_SEIMIN | int | 0 | CoolStep minimum current floor |
| diag0_pin | pin | — | Diagnostic output 0 (active-high for errors/stall) |
| diag1_pin | pin | — | Diagnostic output 1 |

### TMC2208

**Interface:** UART (single-wire)
**Config section:** `[tmc2208 stepper_x]`
**Source:** `klippy/extras/tmc2208.py`

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| uart_pin | pin | required | Single-wire UART pin |
| tx_pin | pin | — | Separate TX pin (if not using single-wire) |
| select_pins | pins | — | Pin(s) to set before UART communication |
| uart_address | int | 0 | UART node address (0–3) |
| run_current | float | required | Motor run current (A RMS) |
| hold_current | float | run_current | Standstill current (A RMS) |
| sense_resistor | float | 0.110 | External sense resistor (Ω) |
| stealthchop_threshold | int | 0 | Velocity threshold for StealthChop→SpreadCycle (0 = disabled) |
| home_current | float | — | Reduced current for homing (Kalico-specific) |
| current_change_dwell_time | float | 0.5 | Delay (s) after current change before homing move |
| driver_IHOLDDELAY | int | 8 | Run-to-hold transition delay |
| driver_TPOWERDOWN | int | 20 | Standstill power-down delay |
| driver_TBL | int | 2 | Blanking time |
| driver_TOFF | int | 3 | Chopper off-time |
| driver_HEND | int | 0 | Hysteresis end value |
| driver_HSTRT | int | 5 | Hysteresis start value |
| driver_PWM_AUTOSCALE | int | 1 | StealthChop auto amplitude |
| driver_PWM_AUTOGRAD | int | 1 | StealthChop auto gradient |
| driver_PWM_FREQ | int | 1 | PWM frequency selection |
| driver_PWM_GRAD | int | 14 | PWM gradient |
| driver_PWM_OFS | int | 36 | PWM offset |
| driver_PWM_REG | int | 8 | PWM regulation rate |
| driver_PWM_LIM | int | 12 | PWM limit |

> **No sensorless homing.** TMC2208 lacks StallGuard — no diag_pin, no virtual endstop support.

### TMC2660

**Interface:** SPI
**Config section:** `[tmc2660 stepper_x]`
**Source:** `klippy/extras/tmc2660.py`

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| spi_speed | int | 4000000 | SPI clock speed (Hz) |
| run_current | float | required | Motor run current (A RMS) |
| sense_resistor | float | required | External sense resistor (Ω) — no default |
| idle_current_percent | int | 100 | Standstill current as % of run_current (0–100) |
| home_current | float | — | Reduced current for homing (Kalico-specific) |
| current_change_dwell_time | float | 0.5 | Delay (s) after current change before homing move |
| driver_TBL | int | 2 | Blanking time |
| driver_TOFF | int | 3 | Chopper off-time |
| driver_HEND | int | 2 | Hysteresis end value |
| driver_HSTRT | int | 3 | Hysteresis start value |
| driver_CHM | int | 0 | Chopper mode |
| driver_RNDTF | int | 0 | Random TOFF mode |
| driver_HDEC | int | 0 | Hysteresis decrement |
| driver_VSENSE | int | 0 | Sense resistor voltage range (0 = high, 1 = low) |
| driver_SDOFF | int | 0 | Step/dir interface disable |
| driver_SFILT | int | 0 | StallGuard filter enable |
| driver_SGT | int | 0 | StallGuard threshold (−64 to +63) |
| driver_RDSEL | int | 0 | Register read select for SG result |

> **No sensorless homing via firmware.** TMC2660 reports StallGuard through SPI read-back (RDSEL), but Kalico does not implement virtual endstop support. Stall detection requires a physical diag pin wired to an MCU input.

---

## Kalico-Specific Additions

These features are present in Kalico but **not in stock Klipper**:

### Native Homing Current (All Drivers)

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| home_current | float | — | Reduced current applied automatically during homing moves |
| current_change_dwell_time | float | 0.5 | Delay (s) after switching current before the homing move begins |

In stock Klipper, reducing current for sensorless homing requires `SET_TMC_CURRENT` macros in homing overrides. Kalico handles this natively — set `home_current` in the `[tmc*]` config block and the firmware manages the current switch and dwell automatically.

### TMC5160/TMC2160 Short-Circuit Tuning

| Parameter | Range | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| driver_s2vs_level | 4–15 | 6 | Short-to-VS detection sensitivity (higher = less sensitive) |
| driver_s2g_level | 2–15 | 6 | Short-to-GND detection sensitivity (higher = less sensitive) |
| driver_shortdelay | 0–1 | 0 | Short detection delay (0 = 750 ns, 1 = 1.5 µs) |
| driver_short_filter | 0–3 | 1 | Short detection filter time (0 = 100 ns, 1 = 1 µs, 2 = 2 µs, 3 = 3 µs) |

These expose TMC5160 DRV_CONF register fields for tuning false-positive short-circuit detection, useful with long motor cables or high-inductance steppers where transients can trigger spurious shutdowns.

### TMC2240 Current Range Control

| Parameter | Range | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| current_range | 0–3 | — | Selects the current measurement range |
| driver_CS | int | — | Direct current scale register value |

These provide finer control over the TMC2240's Rref-based current sensing chain beyond what `run_current` + `rref` alone offer.

---

## G-Code Commands

All TMC drivers in Kalico expose these commands via `TMCCommandHelper`:

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| DUMP_TMC STEPPER=stepper_x | Dump all readable registers for the named TMC driver |
| SET_TMC_FIELD STEPPER=stepper_x FIELD=name VALUE=n | Write a value to a specific register field |
| SET_TMC_CURRENT STEPPER=stepper_x CURRENT=n [HOLDCURRENT=n] | Set run (and optionally hold) current at runtime |
| INIT_TMC STEPPER=stepper_x | Re-initialize the TMC driver (re-send all config registers) |

---

## Sensorless Homing

Kalico supports sensorless homing on drivers with StallGuard:

| Driver | StallGuard Version | Homing Pin Config | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| TMC2130 | SG2 | `diag0_pin` or `diag1_pin` | Set `driver_SGT` threshold |
| TMC2209 | SG4 | `diag_pin` | Set `driver_SGTHRS` threshold |
| TMC2240 | SG4 (+ SG2 via SGT) | `diag0_pin` or `diag1_pin` | `driver_SG4_THRS` or `driver_SGT` |
| TMC5160 | SG2 | `diag0_pin` or `diag1_pin` | Set `driver_SGT` threshold |

**Kalico advantage:** With `home_current` configured, sensorless homing requires no macros — the firmware automatically:
1. Reduces motor current to `home_current`
2. Waits `current_change_dwell_time` seconds
3. Executes the homing move using the StallGuard virtual endstop
4. Restores `run_current` after homing completes

### Tuning Guide (from Kalico docs)

1. Set `hold_current` equal to `run_current` (prevent current changes during tuning)
2. Increase `driver_SGTHRS` (TMC2209) or decrease `driver_SGT` (TMC2130/5160) to increase sensitivity
3. Run `DUMP_TMC` and check `SG_RESULT` at target homing velocity under no-load conditions
4. Set threshold to ~50% of no-load SG_RESULT value
5. Test with actual axis loads; adjust threshold if false triggers occur
6. For CoreXY: both motors home simultaneously — tune both drivers to trigger at similar stall loads

---

## TMC Driver Tuning Reference

### Motor Current

- `run_current`: Start at 60–80% of motor's rated current; increase only if losing steps
- `hold_current`: Kalico docs recommend not setting this (let the driver manage standstill reduction via TPOWERDOWN), but if set, use 30–50% of run_current
- Use `SET_TMC_CURRENT` at runtime to test different values before committing to config

### SpreadCycle vs. StealthChop

- **StealthChop** (default): Silent, voltage-mode PWM. Best at low-to-moderate velocities. Auto-tuned via `PWM_AUTOSCALE` / `PWM_AUTOGRAD`.
- **SpreadCycle**: Current-mode chopper, more precise at high speeds. Audible. Enable by setting `stealthchop_threshold` to the velocity where transition should occur.
- Kalico recommends StealthChop for most 3D printer axes; SpreadCycle for extruders or high-speed applications where torque matters more than noise.

### Common Error Messages

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `Unable to read tmc uart` | UART wiring fault or wrong address | Check wiring, verify `uart_address` matches MS1/MS2 pin state |
| `Unable to write tmc uart` | TX path broken | Check `tx_pin` if using separate TX; check pull-up resistor |
| `TMC reports error: DRV_STATUS` | Short-circuit or overtemperature | Check motor wiring; reduce current; improve cooling |
| `TMC is not ready` | Driver not initialized after power-on | Run `INIT_TMC`; check power supply sequencing |
